channel.title: ssh on My Notebook
channel.description: Recent content in ssh on My Notebook

item.title: SSH setup for GitHub
item.description: Creating a new key I use the names id_rsa_github and and id_rsa_github.pub for my keys, using a unique key from evey host that I need to connect from. Use a keepass long random password. ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "your_email@example.com" To add to the ssh agent use: ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa_github ssh-add -L Configuring GitGub Open the user settings pagem and find SSH and GPG keys on the left. Click on ne SSH key and call it user@host, using your current username that you are using on the host you are connecting from.
